commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Windows-aware repair command invocation, configurable GitHub CLI shims, native path containment checks, and CRLF-tolerant repair/unit test fixtures.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and contributor proof, but not by running current main on Windows in this environment. Current main lacks the Windows command-resolution and batch-launch path, and the PR body includes a redacted Windows terminal excerpt for the repaired path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is queued; the next action is maintainer merge review after exact-head checks finish for an automation and shell-boundary-sensitive change.

target.title: fix: make repair tests portable on Windows
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ClawSweeper's repair validation path and several test fixtures assumed POSIX command lookup, LF-only source reads, executable fake `gh` shims, and `/`-only path containment. That made Windows local validation fail even when the runtime behavior being tested was otherwise portable.

## Why This Change Was Made

This updates repair command execution and GitHub CLI helpers to support explicit tool shims on Windows, makes `.cmd`/`.bat` validation commands run through `cmd.exe` with double-escaped batch arguments, fixes state-root publish containment with platform-native path checks, and normalizes source-fixture reads where assertions inspect tracked files. The test fixtures now use explicit Node-backed fake tools instead of depending on extensionless executables being discoverable through `PATH`.

## User Impact

Maintainers can run the ClawSweeper repair and unit suites from Windows without patching local tool shims or line endings. The state publish path also no longer rejects valid state-root paths on Windows, and Windows batch validation launchers preserve spaces and shell metacharacters in arguments.

## Evidence

Head proofed: `ca6ff16fd5`.

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` passed.
- `node --test test\repair\command-runner.test.ts` passed on Windows, including a real `.cmd` runtime test for arguments with spaces, `&`, parentheses, and `!`.
- `pnpm run test:repair` passed: 569 tests, 569 pass before the escaping follow-up.
- `pnpm run test:unit` passed: 547 tests, 546 pass, 1 skipped before the escaping follow-up.
- `pnpm run build:all` passed.
- `pnpm run lint` passed.
- `pnpm run format:check` passed.
- `pnpm run check` passed after the escaping follow-up, including `check:active-surface`, `check:limits`, `build:all`, `lint`, `test:unit`, `test:repair`, changed/full coverage, and `format:check`. Final summary: 1118 tests, 1117 pass, 1 skipped; coverage 100% lines, branches, and functions for the covered report.
- `codex review -c service_tier='fast' --uncommitted` passed after the escaping follow-up with no actionable correctness issues; the review also ran focused build/test and repair lint checks.

Redacted Windows terminal excerpt for the batch launcher path:

```powershell
> node --test test\repair\command-runner.test.ts
✔ runCommand handles validation output larger than Node's sync spawn default (53.6697ms)
✔ runCommand reports command timeouts with the rendered command (25.3759ms)
✔ runCommand double-escapes Windows batch launcher arguments (2.6751ms)
✔ runCommand preserves Windows batch launcher arguments at runtime (81.7918ms)
ℹ tests 4
ℹ suites 0
ℹ pass 4
ℹ fail 0
ℹ cancelled 0
ℹ skipped 0
ℹ todo 0
ℹ duration_ms 256.5898
```
